'''八縣宿禰神'''（やつあがたすくねのかみ、やのあがたすくねのかみ）は、[[諏訪|州羽地方]]の民間伝承の[[神]]。
== 概要 == 別名・異表記に'''八県宿禰神'''、'''箭津安賀多神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>、'''佐和惠多良六老彦神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>、六老彦神がある。
また[[新海三社神社]]では建御名方神の子・[[興波岐命]]や[[大県神]]の別名としている。
[[貞観]]十年三月九日に正五位を授与される。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。
兄弟に[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]、[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]]<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>がいるが、これらは八縣宿禰神の御子神ともされる<ref>諏訪史料叢書.巻28</ref>。
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[出早雄命]] *[[多満留姫]] *[[建御名方神]]
[[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八縣宿禰神 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 八縣宿禰神 | 別名 = 八県宿禰神、八縣宿祢命、箭津安賀多神、佐和惠多良六老彦神、六老彦神 | 別称 = | 神階 = [[正五位]]下 | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->開拓神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[出早雄命|伊豆早雄命]] | 母 = [[多満留姫]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[健志奈乃命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[霧降宮切久保諏訪神社]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[諏訪氏]] }} '''八縣宿禰神'''（やつあがたすくねのかみ、やのあがたすくねのかみ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地域|州羽地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 別名・異表記に'''八県宿禰神'''、'''箭津安賀多神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>（やつあがたのかみ）、'''佐和惠多良六老彦神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>（さわえたら - 以降読み不明）、'''六老彦神'''がある。

== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。

{{デフォルトソート:やつあかたすくねのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつあかたすくねのかみ]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八縣宿禰神 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 八縣宿禰神 | 別名 = 八県宿禰神、八縣宿祢命、箭津安賀多神、佐和惠多良六老彦神、六老彦神 | 別称 = | 神階 = [[正五位]]下 | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->開拓神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[出早雄命|伊豆早雄命]] | 母 = [[多満留姫]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[健志奈乃命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[霧降宮切久保諏訪神社]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[諏訪氏]] }} '''八縣宿禰神'''（やつあがたすくねのかみ、やのあがたすくねのかみ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地域|州羽地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 別名・異表記に'''八県宿禰神'''、'''箭津安賀多神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>（やつあがたのかみ）、'''八縣宿祢命'''、'''佐和惠多良六老彦神'''<ref>諏訪神社略縁起</ref>（さわえたら - 読み不明 - ひこのかみ）、'''六老彦神'''がある。また[[新海三社神社]]では[[建御名方神]]の子・[[興波岐命]]や[[大県神]]の別名としている。
国史としては『[[日本三代実録]]』を初見とし、もとは[[従五位]]下であったとされる<ref>延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、23頁。</ref>。
[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]十年三月九日に[[正五位]]下を授与される<ref>延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、23頁。</ref>。
== 系譜 == [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。
兄弟に[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]、[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]]<ref>諏訪神社略縁起{{要ページ番号|date=2018年8月}}</ref>がいるが、これらは八縣宿禰神の御子神ともされる<ref>諏訪史料叢書.巻28{{要ページ番号|date=2018年8月}}</ref>。

（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。 兄妹に[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]がおり、これに加え[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]]を記す系図もあるほか守矢実久「健御名方命御系圖」『諏訪神社略縁起』中村甚之助、明治53年、10頁。、[[会津比売神]]を加えたこれら兄妹は八縣宿禰神の御子神ともされる「諏訪下社大祝武居祝系圖略」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、73頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * 旧[[七社明神]]（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]玉川神之原）　現在は廃社 * [[新海三社神社]]（長野県[[佐久市]]田口宮代） * [[五加八幡神社]]（長野県[[上田市]]五加） * [[霧降宮切久保諏訪神社]]（長野県[[白馬村]]北城切久保） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[出早雄命]] *[[多満留姫]] *[[建御名方神]] {{デフォルトソート:やつあかたすくねのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつあかたすくねのかみ]]

revision.comment: More used with only one reference definition: 2 new references and 3 new reference calls.
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 八縣宿禰神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]、[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = | 死没 = | 改名 = | 別名 = 八県宿禰神、八縣宿祢命、箭津安賀多神、佐和惠多良六老彦神、六老彦神 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[諏訪氏]]（[[洲羽国造]]）祖 | 父母 = 父：[[出早雄命|伊豆早雄命]]、母：[[多満留姫]]？ | 兄弟 = [[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]、[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[健志奈乃命]] | 特記事項 = [[正五位]]下 }} '''八縣宿禰神'''（やつあがたすくねのかみ、やのあがたすくねのかみ）は、古代日本の人物または[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 別名・異表記に'''八県宿禰神'''、'''箭津安賀多神'''守矢実久「健御名方命御系圖」『諏訪神社略縁起』中村甚之助、明治53年、10頁。（やつあがたのかみ）、'''八縣宿祢命'''、'''佐和惠多良六老彦神'''（さわえたら - 読み不明 - ひこのかみ）、'''六老彦神'''がある。また[[新海三社神社]]では[[建御名方神]]の子・[[興波岐命]]や[[大県神]]の別名としている。御子神の[[健志奈乃命]]は『修補諏訪氏系図』において建御名方神の御子神の[[蓼科神|健志奈命]]とは別の神としており、[[諏訪氏]]の祖神に位置づけている。 国史としては『[[日本三代実録]]』を初見とし、もとは[[従五位]]下であったとされる延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、23頁。。 [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]十年三月九日に[[正五位]]下を授与される。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。 兄妹に[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]がおり、これに加え[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]]を記す系図もあるほか、[[会津比売神]]を加えたこれら兄妹は八縣宿禰神の御子神ともされる「諏訪下社大祝武居祝系圖略」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、73頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * 旧[[七社明神]]（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]玉川神之原）　現在は廃社 * [[新海三社神社]]（長野県[[佐久市]]田口宮代） * [[五加八幡神社]]（長野県[[上田市]]五加） * [[霧降宮切久保諏訪神社]]（長野県[[白馬村]]北城切久保） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[出早雄命]] *[[多満留姫]] *[[建御名方神]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:やつあかたすくねのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつあかたすくねのかみ]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子・[[出早雄命]]（后は[[多満留姫]]か）の子。 兄妹に[[可毛羽神]]、[[若木比売神]]、[[草奈井比売神]]、[[出早比売命|伊津早姫神]]がおり、これに加え[[大県神|大縣神]]、[[内県神|内安賀多神]]、[[外県神|外安賀多神]]を記す系図もあるほか、[[会津比売神]]を加えたこれら兄妹は八縣宿禰神の御子神ともされる「諏訪下社大祝武居祝系圖略」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、73頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * 旧[[七社明神]]（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]玉川神之原）　現在は廃社 * [[新海三社神社]]（長野県[[佐久市]]田口宮代） * [[五加八幡神社]]（長野県[[上田市]]五加） * [[霧降宮切久保諏訪神社]]（長野県[[白馬村]]北城切久保） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[出早雄命]] *[[多満留姫]] *[[建御名方神]] *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:やつあかたすくねのかみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつあかたすくねのかみ]]

==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]]

{{Infobox noble | name = Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami | image = | image_size = | alt = | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]], [[Yayoi period]] | birth_date = | death_date = | other_names = Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami, Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto, Yatsuagata no Kami, Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami, Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | honorific_prefix = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]]; Mother: [[Tamaruhime]]? | siblings = [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]], [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] | notable_achievements = Promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下) }} {{ill|Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}} (八縣宿禰神[[ja:八縣宿禰神]]) is a figure from ancient Japan, regarded as either a historical person or a [[kami]] ([[Shinto deity|deity]]). == Overview == Alternative names include '''Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami''', '''Yatsuagata no Kami'''Moriya Sanekumi, ''Genealogy of Takeminakata-no-Kami'', in ''Suwa Shrine Abbreviated History'', Nakamura Jin'nosuke, 1910, p. 10. (やつあがたのかみ), '''Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto''', and '''Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami''' (さわえたら - unknown pronunciation - ひこのかみ), and '''Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami'''. At the [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]], this deity is considered a child of [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] and [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]], or an alternate name for [[Oagata-no-Kami]]. The child deity [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] is listed in the ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from the similarly named [[Tateshina-no-Kami]], and is positioned as the ancestral deity of the [[Suwa clan]]. The first historical mention of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami is in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', where the deity held the rank of ''Jūgoi-ge'' (従五位下).Nobukazu Enkawa, ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy,'' in ''Suwa Clan Genealogy, Main Edition,'' Iida Kōtarō, 1921, p. 23. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of the [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] era (860 CE), the deity was promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下). == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya clan]]
(from Moreya-no-Kami to Yakushi-no-Kami)}}]] The child of [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamaruhime]] (possibly the mother). Siblings include [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], and [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]]. Some genealogies add [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], and [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] as siblings. In other versions, these deities are listed as the children of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami.''Summary Genealogy of Suwa Lower Shrine High Priests,'' in ''Suwa Historical Documents, Volume 28,'' Suwa Education Association, 1936, p. 73. == Shrines enshrining Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami == * Former [[Shichisha Myojin]] (Kaminohara, Tamagawa, [[Chino, Nagano]]) – now defunct * [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]] (Taguchi Miyashiro, [[Saku, Nagano]]) * [[Goka Hachiman Shrine]] (Goka, [[Ueda, Nagano]]) * [[Kirigubo Suwa Shrine]] (Kitashiro Kirikubo, [[Hakuba, Nagano]]) == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tamaruhime]] * [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] * [[List of figures from pre-Asuka period Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Figures from ancient Japan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa faith|Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami]] {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]]

Some genealogies add [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], and [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] as siblings. In other versions, these deities are listed as the children of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami.''Summary Genealogy of Suwa Lower Shrine High Priests,'' in ''Suwa Historical Documents, Volume 28,'' Suwa Education Association, 1936, p. 73. == Shrines enshrining Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami == * Former [[Shichisha Myojin]] (Kaminohara, Tamagawa, [[Chino, Nagano]]) – now defunct * [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]] (Taguchi Miyashiro, [[Saku, Nagano]]) * [[Goka Hachiman Shrine]] (Goka, [[Ueda, Nagano]]) * [[Kirigubo Suwa Shrine]] (Kitashiro Kirikubo, [[Hakuba, Nagano]]) == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tamaruhime]] * [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] * [[List of figures from pre-Asuka period Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Figures from ancient Japan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa faith|Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami]] {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]]

In other versions, these deities are listed as the children of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami.''Summary Genealogy of Suwa Lower Shrine High Priests,'' in ''Suwa Historical Documents, Volume 28,'' Suwa Education Association, 1936, p. 73. == Shrines enshrining Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami == * Former [[Shichisha Myojin]] (Kaminohara, Tamagawa, [[Chino, Nagano]]) – now defunct * [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]] (Taguchi Miyashiro, [[Saku, Nagano]]) * [[Goka Hachiman Shrine]] (Goka, [[Ueda, Nagano]]) * [[Kirigubo Suwa Shrine]] (Kitashiro Kirikubo, [[Hakuba, Nagano]]) == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tamaruhime]] * [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] * [[List of figures from pre-Asuka period Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Figures from ancient Japan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa faith|Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami]] {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami | image = | image_size = | alt = | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]], [[Yayoi period]] | birth_date = | death_date = | other_names = Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami, Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto, Yatsuagata no Kami, Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami, Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | honorific_prefix = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]]; Mother: [[Tamaruhime]]? | siblings = [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]], [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] | notable_achievements = Promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下) }} {{ill|Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}} (八縣宿禰神[[ja:八縣宿禰神]]) is a figure from ancient Japan, regarded as either a historical person or a [[kami]] ([[Shinto deity|deity]]). == Overview == Alternative names include '''Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami''', '''Yatsuagata no Kami'''Moriya Sanekumi, ''Genealogy of Takeminakata-no-Kami'', in ''Suwa Shrine Abbreviated History'', Nakamura Jin'nosuke, 1910, p. 10. (やつあがたのかみ), '''Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto''', and '''Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami''' (さわえたら - unknown pronunciation - ひこのかみ), and '''Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami'''. At the [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]], this deity is considered a child of [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] and [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]], or an alternate name for [[Oagata-no-Kami]]. The child deity [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] is listed in the ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from the similarly named [[Tateshina-no-Kami]], and is positioned as the ancestral deity of the [[Suwa clan]]. The first historical mention of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami is in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', where the deity held the rank of ''Jūgoi-ge'' (従五位下).Nobukazu Enkawa, ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy,'' in ''Suwa Clan Genealogy, Main Edition,'' Iida Kōtarō, 1921, p. 23. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of the [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] era (860 CE), the deity was promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下). == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya clan]]
(from Moreya-no-Kami to Yakushi-no-Kami)}}]] The child of [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamaruhime]] (possibly the mother). Siblings include [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], and [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]]. Some genealogies add [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], and [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] as siblings. In other versions, these deities are listed as the children of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami.''Summary Genealogy of Suwa Lower Shrine High Priests,'' in ''Suwa Historical Documents, Volume 28,'' Suwa Education Association, 1936, p. 73. == Shrines enshrining Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami == * Former [[Shichisha Myojin]] (Kaminohara, Tamagawa, [[Chino, Nagano]]) – now defunct * [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]] (Taguchi Miyashiro, [[Saku, Nagano]]) * [[Goka Hachiman Shrine]] (Goka, [[Ueda, Nagano]]) * [[Kirigubo Suwa Shrine]] (Kitashiro Kirikubo, [[Hakuba, Nagano]]) == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tamaruhime]] * [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] * [[List of figures from pre-Asuka period Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Figures from ancient Japan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa faith|Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami]] {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox noble | name = Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami | image = | image_size = | alt = | era = [[Ancient Japan|Ancient]], [[Yayoi period]] | birth_date = | death_date = | other_names = Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami, Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto, Yatsuagata no Kami, Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami, Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | honorific_prefix = | burial_place = | rank = | lord = | clan = | parents = Father: [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]]; Mother: [[Tamaruhime]]? | siblings = [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]], [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] | spouse = | children = [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] | notable_achievements = Promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下) }} {{ill|Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami|ja|八縣宿禰神}} (八縣宿禰神[[ja:八縣宿禰神]]) is a figure from ancient Japan, regarded as either a historical person or a [[kami]] ([[Shinto deity|deity]]). == Overview == Alternative names include '''Yatsukata Sukune-no-Kami''', '''Yatsuagata no Kami'''Moriya Sanekumi, ''Genealogy of Takeminakata-no-Kami'', in ''Suwa Shrine Abbreviated History'', Nakamura Jin'nosuke, 1910, p. 10. (やつあがたのかみ), '''Yatsugata Sukune-no-Mikoto''', and '''Sawaetaru Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami''' (さわえたら - unknown pronunciation - ひこのかみ), and '''Rokuoro-hiko-no-Kami'''. At the [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]], this deity is considered a child of [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] and [[Yasakatome-no-Kami]], or an alternate name for [[Oagata-no-Kami]]. The child deity [[Kenshinano-no-Mikoto]] is listed in the ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from the similarly named [[Tateshina-no-Kami]], and is positioned as the ancestral deity of the [[Suwa clan]]. The first historical mention of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami is in the ''[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', where the deity held the rank of ''Jūgoi-ge'' (従五位下).Nobukazu Enkawa, ''Supplement to the Suwa Clan Genealogy,'' in ''Suwa Clan Genealogy, Main Edition,'' Iida Kōtarō, 1921, p. 23. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of the [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] era (860 CE), the deity was promoted to ''Shōgoi-ge'' (正五位下). == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|The first five generations of the [[Moriya clan]]
(from Moreya-no-Kami to Yakushi-no-Kami)}}]] The child of [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] and [[Tamaruhime]] (possibly the mother). Siblings include [[Kamohane-no-Kami]], [[Wakakihime-no-Kami]], [[Kusanaehime-no-Kami]], and [[Izu Hayahime-no-Kami]]. Some genealogies add [[Oagata-no-Kami]], [[Uchiagata-no-Kami]], and [[Sotoagata-no-Kami]] as siblings. In other versions, these deities are listed as the children of Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami.''Summary Genealogy of Suwa Lower Shrine High Priests,'' in ''Suwa Historical Documents, Volume 28,'' Suwa Education Association, 1936, p. 73. == Shrines enshrining Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami == * Former [[Shichisha Myojin]] (Kaminohara, Tamagawa, [[Chino, Nagano]]) – now defunct * [[Shinkai Sansha Shrine]] (Taguchi Miyashiro, [[Saku, Nagano]]) * [[Goka Hachiman Shrine]] (Goka, [[Ueda, Nagano]]) * [[Kirigubo Suwa Shrine]] (Kitashiro Kirikubo, [[Hakuba, Nagano]]) == References == {{Reflist}} == See also == * [[Izu Hayao-no-Mikoto]] * [[Tamaruhime]] * [[Takeminakata-no-Kami]] * [[List of figures from pre-Asuka period Japan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Figures from ancient Japan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa faith|Yatsugata Sukune-no-Kami]] {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八縣宿禰神]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Yatsugatake Sukune-no-Kami]]

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].

The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].
== Overview == Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].
The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}} , who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}
== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami == * Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八縣宿禰神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八縣宿禰神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八縣宿禰神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q56347860]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami (八縣宿禰神) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] / [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = | death_date = | other_names = 八県宿禰神, 八縣宿祢命, 箭津安賀多神, 佐和惠多良六老彦神, 六老彦神 | title = | deity_of = | family = | parents = Father: {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}} , Mother: {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}} | siblings = {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = {{ill|Shogo-i|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower }}
'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].
== Overview == Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].
The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}} , who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}
== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami == * Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}} * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] * {{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}} , who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or [[:en:Deity|kami]].
== Overview == Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]].
The deity first appears in the historical record in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}} , who is himself the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}
== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami == * Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}}.

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}.

The deity first appears in the historical record in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan}} 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} (Taguchi Miyashiro, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja|五加八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goka Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Goka, Ueda City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami''' (八縣宿禰神) is an ancient Japanese figure or {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}}.

== Overview ==
Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is also referred to as '''八県宿禰神''', '''箭津安賀多神''' (Yatsuakata no Kami), '''八縣宿祢命''', or '''佐和惠多良六老彦神''' (Saweetara - exact reading unknown - Hiko no Kami). At the {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}}, Yatsuakata is considered either the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} or a name for {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The offspring, {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|健志奈乃命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is described in the ''Suho Suwa Clan Genealogy'' as distinct from {{ill|Takeshina no Mikoto|ja|蓼科神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a child of Takeminakata. He is recognized as the progenitor deity of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}.

The deity first appears in the historical record in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as holding the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower. On the 9th day of the 3rd month of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan}} 10 (868 CE), Yatsuakata was elevated to the Rank of {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1985|p=23}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|200px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami is the child of {{ill|Izuhayao no Mikoto|ja|出早雄命|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
, who is himself the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. Yatsuakata's mother may have been {{ill|Tamaru-hime|ja|多満留姫|1=Tamaru-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru-hime|qq=}}. He had siblings, including {{ill|Kamohagami|ja|可毛羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamohagami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493216}}, {{ill|Wakakihime|ja|若木比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakakihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135492776}}, {{ill|Kusanaihime|ja|草奈井比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanaihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493035}}, {{ill|Izuhayahime|ja|伊津早姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhayahime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as well as {{ill|Okata no Kami|ja|大縣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Uchiakata no Kami|ja|内安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sotakata no Kami|ja|外安賀多神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotakata no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Some genealogies also include {{ill|Aizu-hime-no-Kami|ja|会津比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizuhime no Kami|qq=}}, described as either a sibling or offspring of Yatsuakata.{{Sfn|Jinosuke|1912|p=73}}

== Shrines Dedicated to Yatsuakata Sukune no Kami ==
* Former {{ill|Shichisha Myojin|ja|七社明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisha Myojin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (now defunct, located in Tamagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)

* {{ill|Kirigafuri no Miya|ja|霧降宮切久保諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirigafuri no Miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kirikubo, Hakuba Village, Nagano Prefecture)
